title: Strategic Management and Planning
description: Understand strategic planning processes and how to audit strategic initiatives

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: Internal auditors need business acumen to effectively evaluate risks and add value. Understanding strategy helps you assess whether controls support organizational objectives. Part 3 tests your business knowledge!

sections.title: Strategic Planning Process
sections.content:
**Strategic planning is the process of:**

1. **Defining mission and vision**
• Mission: Why we exist
• Vision: Where we're going

2. **Analyzing environment**
• External opportunities and threats
• Internal strengths and weaknesses

3. **Setting objectives**
• Long-term goals aligned with mission
• SMART objectives

4. **Developing strategies**
• How to achieve objectives
• Competitive positioning

5. **Implementation and monitoring**
• Action plans
• Performance measurement

sections.title: Porter's Competitive Forces
sections.content:
**Five Forces Model:**

**1. Threat of New Entrants**
• Barriers to entry
• Capital requirements

**2. Bargaining Power of Suppliers**
• Supplier concentration
• Switching costs

**3. Bargaining Power of Buyers**
• Customer concentration
• Price sensitivity

**4. Threat of Substitutes**
• Alternative products
• Switching costs

**5. Industry Rivalry**
• Number of competitors
• Growth rate

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: Porter's Five Forces
sections.content:
**"TRIBES"**:

**T**hreat of new entrants
**R**ivalry among competitors
**I**nfluence (bargaining power) of buyers
**B**argaining power of suppliers
**E**xistence of **S**ubstitutes

*The 5 forces that shape industry competition!*

sections.title: Auditing Strategy
sections.content:
**Internal audit can assess:**

• Alignment of strategy with mission/vision
• Quality of strategic planning process
• Risk identification in strategic initiatives
• Validity of assumptions
• Monitoring and KPI systems
• Resource allocation for strategic priorities

**Strategic risks:**
• Failed strategy execution
• Disruptive technologies
• Competitive shifts
• Regulatory changes

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Strategic planning: Mission → Analysis → Objectives → Strategy → Implementation
SWOT: Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats
Porter's Five Forces analyze competitive environment
Generic strategies: Cost leadership, Differentiation, Focus
IA can audit strategy process and strategic risk management

title: Organizational Structure and Behavior
description: Understand organizational structures, culture, and human behavior concepts

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: Understanding how organizations work helps auditors navigate politics, assess culture, and understand why controls fail. Organizational behavior knowledge is tested directly on Part 3!

sections.title: Organizational Structures
rows: Separate units by product, geography, customer

sections.title: Organizational Culture
sections.content:
**Definition:**
Shared values, beliefs, and practices that guide behavior.

**Elements:**
• Visible artifacts (logos, office layout)
• Espoused values (stated beliefs)
• Underlying assumptions (unconscious beliefs)

**Strong culture:**
• Clear values widely shared
• Consistent behavior
• Can be positive or negative

**Culture impacts:**
• Control effectiveness
• Risk appetite
• Ethical behavior

sections.title: Motivation Theories
sections.content:
**Maslow's Hierarchy:**
• Physiological → Safety → Social → Esteem → Self-actualization

**Herzberg's Two-Factor:**
• Hygiene factors (salary, conditions) - prevent dissatisfaction
• Motivators (achievement, recognition) - create satisfaction

**Expectancy Theory:**
• Effort → Performance → Outcome
• Motivation = Expectancy × Instrumentality × Valence

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: Maslow's Hierarchy
sections.content:
**Bottom to Top:**

**P**hysiological - Food, water, shelter
**S**afety - Security, stability
**S**ocial - Belonging, love
**E**steem - Recognition, status
**S**elf-actualization - Reaching potential

*"Please Stay Safe, Earn Success"*

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Structure types: Functional, Divisional, Matrix, Flat, Network
Culture = shared values, beliefs, practices
Motivation theories: Maslow, Herzberg, Expectancy
Leadership styles vary by situation and team
Culture significantly impacts control effectiveness

title: Business Processes and Operations
description: Understand common business processes and operational management concepts

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: Auditors must understand the processes they audit! Operations knowledge helps you identify what can go wrong and where controls are needed. This is highly practical exam content.

sections.title: Business Process Fundamentals
sections.content:
**A business process is:**
A set of activities that transforms inputs into outputs to achieve a goal.

**Process elements:**
• Inputs (materials, information)
• Activities (steps performed)
• Outputs (products, services)
• Controls (checkpoints, approvals)

**Process categories:**
• Core/operational (value-creating)
• Support (enabling)
• Management (directing)

sections.title: Quality Management
sections.content:
**Total Quality Management (TQM):**
• Customer focus
• Continuous improvement
• Employee involvement
• Process-centered approach

**Six Sigma:**
• Data-driven quality improvement
• DMAIC: Define, Measure, Analyze, Improve, Control
• Goal: 3.4 defects per million opportunities

**ISO 9000:**
• International quality standards
• Focus on QMS (Quality Management System)
• Certification demonstrates conformance

sections.title: Supply Chain Management
sections.content:
**Supply chain encompasses:**
• Suppliers → Manufacturing → Distribution → Customer

**Key considerations:**
• Supplier selection and monitoring
• Inventory management
• Logistics and transportation
• Demand forecasting

**Risks:**
• Supply disruption
• Quality issues
• Lead time variability
• Third-party dependency

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: DMAIC
sections.content:
**D**efine - What's the problem?
**M**easure - How bad is it?
**A**nalyze - Why is it happening?
**I**mprove - What's the solution?
**C**ontrol - How do we sustain it?

*"Don't Make Any Improvement Carelessly"*

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Processes transform inputs to outputs
Key cycles: Revenue, Procurement, Payroll, Inventory, Fixed Assets
TQM focuses on customer and continuous improvement
Six Sigma: DMAIC for data-driven improvement
Supply chain risks include disruption and third-party dependency

title: Project Management
description: Understand project management principles and auditing project controls

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: Projects often fail - over budget, late, or not meeting objectives. Internal audit adds value by assessing project governance and controls. Project management knowledge helps you audit these critical initiatives!

sections.title: Project Management Basics
sections.content:
**A project is:**
A temporary endeavor to create a unique product, service, or result.

**Key characteristics:**
• Defined beginning and end
• Unique deliverables
• Progressive elaboration
• Constraints (scope, time, cost)

**Triple Constraint:**
Scope ↔ Time ↔ Cost
(Change one, affects others)

sections.title: Project Governance
sections.content:
**Governance structure:**
• Steering Committee (oversight)
• Project Sponsor (executive accountability)
• Project Manager (daily management)
• Project Team (execution)

**PMO (Project Management Office):**
• Standardizes processes
• Provides tools and training
• Portfolio oversight
• Resource management

sections.title: Project Risks
sections.content:
**Common project risks:**

• **Scope creep** - Uncontrolled scope expansion
• **Resource constraints** - Insufficient people/skills
• **Schedule delays** - Missed deadlines
• **Budget overruns** - Cost increases
• **Technical risks** - Technology failures
• **Stakeholder issues** - Lack of engagement

**Risk responses:**
• Avoid, Transfer, Mitigate, Accept

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: Triple Constraint
sections.content:
**"STC Triangle"**

**S**cope - What will be delivered
**T**ime - When it will be done
**C**ost - What it will cost

*Change one corner, the others are affected!*

**Quality** sits in the middle - constrained by all three.

sections.title: Auditing Projects
sections.content:
**IA can assess:**

• Project governance structure
• Planning adequacy
• Risk management
• Change control process
• Progress monitoring
• Stakeholder management
• Budget and resource controls
• Post-implementation reviews

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Projects are temporary with unique deliverables
Triple constraint: Scope, Time, Cost
Phases: Initiation, Planning, Execution, Monitoring, Closing
Common risks: Scope creep, resource/schedule/budget issues
IA audits governance, controls, and risk management

title: Information Security Fundamentals
description: Understand core information security concepts and the CIA triad

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: Information security is critical in today's digital world. Internal auditors must understand security concepts to evaluate IT controls and assess cyber risks. This is 25% of Part 3!

sections.title: The CIA Triad
sections.content:
**Three pillars of information security:**

**Confidentiality**
• Protect information from unauthorized access
• Controls: Encryption, access controls, classification

**Integrity**
• Ensure information is accurate and complete
• Controls: Hashing, checksums, audit trails

**Availability**
• Ensure information is accessible when needed
• Controls: Redundancy, backups, disaster recovery

sections.title: Common Threats
sections.content:
**External threats:**
• Hackers and cybercriminals
• Malware (viruses, ransomware)
• Phishing and social engineering
• Denial of service attacks
• Advanced persistent threats (APT)

**Internal threats:**
• Malicious insiders
• Accidental disclosure
• Policy violations
• Privilege misuse

**Environmental threats:**
• Natural disasters
• Power failures
• Hardware failures

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: CIA Triad
sections.content:
**C**onfidentiality - Only authorized see it
**I**ntegrity - It's accurate and unchanged
**A**vailability - It's there when needed

*Not to be confused with Certified Internal Auditor!*

**Memory:** "CIA protects your data from spies (C), lies (I), and downtime (A)"

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: CIA Triad: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability
Additional principles: Authentication, Authorization, Non-repudiation
Threats: External (hackers), Internal (insiders), Environmental
Control types: Preventive, Detective, Corrective, Deterrent, Compensating
Defense in depth = multiple layers of security

title: Access Control and Identity Management
description: Understand access control models, authentication, and authorization

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: Access control is the first line of defense! Understanding authentication methods and access models helps auditors evaluate whether the right people have the right access to the right resources.

sections.title: Authentication Factors
sections.content:
**Something you KNOW:**
• Passwords, PINs, security questions

**Something you HAVE:**
• Smart cards, tokens, mobile devices

**Something you ARE:**
• Fingerprints, facial recognition, iris scan

**Multi-factor authentication (MFA):**
Combines two or more different factor types
(Two passwords = NOT MFA, both are "know")

sections.title: Access Control Models
rows: Owner controls access to their resources

sections.title: Access Control Principles
sections.content:
**Least Privilege:**
• Only access needed to perform job
• Minimize attack surface

**Need to Know:**
• Access only to information required
• Even with clearance, need justification

**Separation of Duties:**
• No single person controls entire process
• Requires collusion for fraud

**Defense in Depth:**
• Multiple layers of security
• If one fails, others protect

sections.title: Identity Management Lifecycle
sections.content:
**Provisioning:**
• Create accounts when hired
• Assign appropriate access

**Maintenance:**
• Update access as roles change
• Periodic access reviews

**De-provisioning:**
• Remove access promptly on termination
• Disable rather than delete (audit trail)

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: Access Control Models
sections.content:
**DAC** - "D" for Discretionary, owner decides
**MAC** - "M" for Military, labels/clearance
**RBAC** - "R" for Role, job-based
**ABAC** - "A" for Attributes, policy-based

*"Don't Make Rigid Access Barriers" → DAC, MAC, RBAC, ABAC*

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Authentication factors: Know, Have, Are
MFA requires different factor types
Access models: DAC, MAC, RBAC, ABAC
Principles: Least privilege, Need to know, Separation of duties
Lifecycle: Provision, Maintain, De-provision

title: Network and Data Security
description: Understand network security controls and data protection measures

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: Networks connect everything - and are prime targets for attack. Data protection including encryption is essential. Auditors must understand these controls to evaluate cyber risk!

sections.title: Encryption Concepts
sections.content:
**Symmetric encryption:**
• Same key encrypts and decrypts
• Fast, efficient
• Key distribution challenge
• Examples: AES, DES, 3DES

**Asymmetric encryption:**
• Public key encrypts, Private key decrypts
• Slower but solves key distribution
• Examples: RSA, ECC

**Hashing:**
• One-way function, cannot reverse
• Verifies integrity
• Examples: SHA-256, MD5

sections.title: Data Protection
sections.content:
**Data at Rest:**
• Full disk encryption
• Database encryption
• File-level encryption

**Data in Transit:**
• TLS/SSL for web traffic
• VPN for network traffic
• Encrypted email

**Data in Use:**
• Secure enclaves
• Memory encryption
• Application-level protection

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: Encryption Types
sections.content:
**Symmetric** - "S" for Same key (one key)
**Asymmetric** - "A" for Actually two keys (public/private)
**Hashing** - "H" for Hash (one-way, can't reverse)

*Symmetric is faster, Asymmetric solves key sharing*

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Network controls: Firewalls, IDS/IPS, VPN, DMZ
Symmetric = one key, Asymmetric = two keys
Protect data at rest, in transit, and in use
Data classification drives protection level
Encryption is essential for confidentiality

title: IT Governance and Operations
description: Understand IT governance frameworks and operational management

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: IT underpins every business process. Understanding IT governance frameworks and operations helps auditors evaluate whether IT supports organizational objectives and manages risks effectively.

sections.title: IT Governance
sections.content:
**Definition:**
The processes that ensure IT supports and enables achievement of enterprise objectives.

**Key elements:**
• Strategic alignment with business
• Value delivery from IT investments
• Risk management
• Resource optimization
• Performance measurement

sections.title: COBIT Overview
sections.content:
**COBIT (Control Objectives for Information Technology):**

**Governance objectives:**
• Evaluate, Direct, Monitor (EDM)

**Management objectives:**
• Align, Plan, Organize (APO)
• Build, Acquire, Implement (BAI)
• Deliver, Service, Support (DSS)
• Monitor, Evaluate, Assess (MEA)

**Design factors** customize implementation to the organization.

sections.title: ITIL Service Management
sections.content:
**ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library):**

**Service Value System practices:**
• Service desk
• Incident management
• Problem management
• Change enablement
• Configuration management
• Release management

**Key concept:** IT delivers VALUE through SERVICES.

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: COBIT Domains
sections.content:
**Governance:** EDM - "Executives Direct and Monitor"

**Management:**
• **A**PO - Align, Plan, Organize
• **B**AI - Build, Acquire, Implement
• **D**SS - Deliver, Service, Support
• **M**EA - Monitor, Evaluate, Assess

*"Always Build, Deliver, and Measure" - ABDM*

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: IT governance ensures IT supports business objectives
COBIT: Governance (EDM) and Management (APO, BAI, DSS, MEA)
ITIL focuses on IT service management
Common practices: Incident, Problem, Change management
Frameworks can be customized to organizational needs

title: IT General Controls
description: Understand IT general controls and their audit

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: IT general controls (ITGCs) are the foundation for reliable application controls. Weak ITGCs undermine ALL automated controls. Auditors must understand and test these controls!

sections.title: Logical Access Controls
sections.content:
**Key controls:**

• User access provisioning and approval
• Password complexity requirements
• Periodic access reviews
• Privileged access management
• Terminated user removal
• Segregation of duties

**Common issues:**
• Excessive access
• Shared accounts
• Orphan accounts
• Privileged access abuse

sections.title: Change Management Controls
sections.content:
**Change control process:**

1. Request and document change
2. Impact and risk assessment
3. Testing in non-production
4. Approval before production
5. Implementation with rollback plan
6. Post-implementation review

**Key principle:**
Developers should NOT have access to production!
(Separation of environments)

sections.title: Computer Operations Controls
sections.content:
**Operational controls:**

• Job scheduling and monitoring
• Console logs and alerts
• Backup and recovery
• Problem/incident management
• Capacity planning
• Environmental controls (power, HVAC)

**Backup types:**
• Full - Everything
• Incremental - Changed since last backup
• Differential - Changed since last full

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: ITGC Categories
sections.content:
**"L-C-C-P"**

**L**ogical Access - WHO accesses
**C**hange Management - HOW changes happen
**C**omputer Operations - HOW systems run
**P**rogram Development - HOW systems are built

*"Learn Controls, Control Problems"*

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: ITGCs: Logical Access, Change Management, Operations, Development
Weak ITGCs undermine all automated controls
Separation: Developers should not access production
Access reviews should be periodic and documented
Change requires testing and approval before production

title: Financial Accounting Concepts
description: Understand financial statements, accounting principles, and key ratios

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: Internal auditors must understand financial statements to evaluate controls over financial reporting. Financial analysis skills help identify unusual patterns and assess organizational health!

sections.title: Primary Financial Statements
rows: Financial position at a point in time

sections.title: Fundamental Accounting Principles
sections.content:
**Accrual Basis:**
• Recognize revenue when earned
• Recognize expenses when incurred
• Not when cash changes hands

**Key Principles:**
• **Going concern** - Assume continued operations
• **Matching** - Match expenses to related revenue
• **Conservatism** - When uncertain, be cautious
• **Materiality** - Focus on significant items
• **Consistency** - Same methods over time

sections.title: Understanding the Cash Flow Statement
sections.content:
**Operating Activities:**
• Day-to-day business operations
• Net income adjusted for non-cash items
• Changes in working capital

**Investing Activities:**
• Purchase/sale of long-term assets
• Investment acquisitions/disposals

**Financing Activities:**
• Borrowing/repaying debt
• Issuing/repurchasing stock
• Dividend payments

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: Accounting Equation
sections.content:
**A = L + E**

**A**ssets = **L**iabilities + **E**quity

*What we HAVE = What we OWE + What we OWN*

This ALWAYS balances - that's why it's called a Balance Sheet!

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Four financial statements: Balance Sheet, Income Statement, Cash Flow, Equity
Accrual basis: Recognize when earned/incurred, not when cash moves
A = L + E (Assets = Liabilities + Equity)
Key ratios: Liquidity, Profitability, Leverage, Activity
Cash flow has three sections: Operating, Investing, Financing

title: Managerial Accounting
description: Understand cost accounting, budgeting, and performance measurement

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.content: Managerial accounting provides information for internal decision-making. Understanding cost behavior, budgets, and variances helps auditors evaluate operational performance and financial controls!

sections.title: Cost-Volume-Profit Analysis
sections.content:
**Contribution Margin:**
Sales - Variable Costs = CM

**Break-Even Point:**
Fixed Costs / CM per unit = Break-even units
Fixed Costs / CM ratio = Break-even sales $

**Target Profit:**
(Fixed Costs + Target Profit) / CM per unit = Required units

sections.title: Budgeting Process
sections.content:
**Master budget components:**

**Operating budgets:**
• Sales budget (starting point)
• Production budget
• Direct materials, labor, overhead
• Operating expense

**Financial budgets:**
• Cash budget
• Budgeted balance sheet
• Budgeted income statement

**Budget types:**
• Static - Fixed targets
• Flexible - Adjusts for volume
• Zero-based - Start from zero each period

sections.title: 🧠 Memory Aid: Break-Even
sections.content:
**Break-Even = Fixed Costs ÷ CM**

*Think: How much contribution margin do I need to COVER my fixed costs?*

**CM = Sales - Variable Costs**
*What's left after variable costs to cover fixed and profit*

sections.title: Key Takeaways
content: Costs: Fixed, Variable, Mixed; Direct, Indirect; Relevant, Sunk
Break-even: Fixed Costs / Contribution Margin
Master budget starts with sales forecast
Flexible budgets adjust for actual volume
Variances: Price/Rate and Quantity/Efficiency
